answers: Compare prices, wait for sales, read reviews, set budget, check return policies.

AI lets machines think, learn, and solve problems like humans. It powers everything from voice assistants to self-driving cars.

AI = making machines smart. It covers ML, deep learning, NLP, computer vision, and robotics. It's reshaping every industry today.

Climate change is real - Earth's temperature is rising due to greenhouse gases from burning fossil fuels, deforestation, and industrial activity.
Go green: reduce, reuse, recycle. Use LED bulbs, eat less meat, avoid single-use plastic, and support eco-friendly brands. Every action counts!
Global warming = Earth heating up due to CO2 and other greenhouse gases. Effects: melting ice, rising seas, extreme weather. We must act now.
Parenting basics: be patient, listen to your child, set boundaries with love, spend quality time, and lead by example. No parent is perfect!
For new parents: sleep when baby sleeps, don't compare with others, ask for help, and trust your instincts. You're doing better than you think.
Child development tip: read to your kids daily, limit screen time, encourage curiosity, let them play freely, and praise effort not just results.
Recommended books: "The Whole-Brain Child", "How to Talk So Kids Will Listen", "Parenting with Love and Logic". Great for all ages!
The universe is HUGE - observable universe is 93 billion light-years across, with over 2 trillion galaxies. And it's still expanding!
Our solar system has 8 planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune. Jupiter is the largest, Mercury the smallest.
A black hole is a region where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape. They form when massive stars collapse.
Space facts: the Sun is 1.3 million times bigger than Earth, a day on Venus is longer than its year, and Saturn's rings are mostly ice and rock!
Writing tip: write every day, even if it's bad. Read a lot, edit ruthlessly, and remember - the first draft is never perfect. Just get words down!
For blogging: pick a niche, write consistently, use SEO basics, promote on social media, and focus on helping your readers solve problems.
Creative writing: show don't tell, use strong verbs, create vivid characters, and read widely across genres for inspiration. Write what excites you!
Essay structure: Introduction (hook + thesis), Body (3 points with evidence), Conclusion (restate + final thought). Keep paragraphs focused and clear.
Bitcoin is a decentralized digital currency - no bank or government controls it. Transactions are verified by a network using blockchain technology.
Crypto investing tip: only invest what you can afford to lose. Start small, research the project, use trusted exchanges (Binance, Coinbase), and HODL.
Blockchain = a digital ledger that records transactions across many computers. It's transparent, secure, and the backbone of all cryptocurrencies.
Is crypto safe? The technology is secure, but the market is highly volatile. Never invest blindly, avoid scam coins, and always use 2FA on exchanges.
Time management golden rule: plan your day the night before. List your top 3 priorities and tackle the hardest one first (eat the frog).
Stop procrastinating: use the 2-minute rule - if it takes less than 2 minutes, do it now. Break big tasks into tiny steps and just start.
Daily routine tip: wake up at the same time daily, time-block your calendar, batch similar tasks, and protect your most productive hours from distractions.
Get organized: use a planner or Notion, declutter your workspace, set deadlines for everything, and review your week every Sunday. Structure = freedom.

questions: how to fix things at home
answers: Easy DIY: build a bookshelf, paint a room, create a photo wall, make candles, or build a simple desk organizer. YouTube tutorials help a lot!
Home fix basics: learn to change a lightbulb, unclog a drain, fix a leaky tap, and paint walls. These save money and build confidence!
Craft ideas: handmade cards, resin art, macrame plant hangers, tie-dye shirts, or custom phone cases. Great for gifts and relaxation!
DIY tip: start with simple projects, watch tutorials first, have the right tools ready, and don't rush. Measure twice, cut once!
Start with basic poses: Mountain, Downward Dog, Warrior I & II, Child's Pose, and Cobra. Just 15 minutes daily makes a big difference!
Yoga benefits: improved flexibility, reduced stress, better posture, stronger core, mental clarity, and better sleep. Good for body and mind!
Beginner tip: don't compare yourself to others. Yoga is about YOUR body and YOUR progress. Use a mat, wear comfortable clothes, and breathe deeply.
Follow YouTube channels like Yoga With Adriene for free guided sessions. Start with 10-15 minute beginner flows and build up gradually.
Top podcasts: Lex Fridman (tech/science), Huberman Lab (health), The Joe Rogan Experience (variety), and How I Built This (business stories).
Start a podcast: pick a niche, get a decent USB mic (Blue Yeti), use Anchor.fm (free hosting), record regularly, and promote on social media.
Podcasting tips: keep episodes focused, have good audio quality, be consistent with your schedule, and engage with your listeners on social media.
For learning: try TED Radio Hour, Freakonomics, or Darknet Diaries (cybersecurity). For fun: Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend, SmartLess.
SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to manage relational databases. Learn SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE - these cover 80% of daily use.
SQL vs NoSQL: SQL (MySQL, PostgreSQL) = structured, tables, relations. NoSQL (MongoDB, Redis) = flexible, documents, fast. Choose based on your data.
Start with MySQL or PostgreSQL for relational data. For NoSQL, try MongoDB. Practice on SQLBolt.com or W3Schools SQL tutorial - both free!
MongoDB stores data as JSON-like documents - flexible and great for apps with changing data structures. Perfect for Node.js backends and modern web apps.

questions: how to push code to github
answers: Git is a version control system - it tracks changes in your code so you can go back, collaborate, and manage projects. Every developer must learn it!
Essential git commands: git init, git add, git commit, git push, git pull, git branch, git merge. These 7 commands handle 90% of daily work.
GitHub workflow: create repo, clone it, make changes, git add, git commit, git push. For collaboration: fork, branch, pull request, merge.
Git tip: commit often with clear messages, use branches for features, never push directly to main, and learn git log and git diff for debugging.
